etymology.description: semantic 彡(彡) (shān, "pattern") + phonetic 景(景) (jǐng, "scenery") -> shadow or image
etymology.interpretation: 影 combines the visual aspect of 彡(彡) (shān, "pattern") with the sound and concept of 景(景) (jǐng, "scenery"), to denote intangible representations such as shadows and images, which are both detailed patterns influenced by light, similar to how scenery can provide a view or an image.

examples.chinese: 他的影子在墙上显得很长(他的影子在牆上顯得很長)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǐngzi zài qiáng shàng xiǎnde hěn cháng.
examples.english: His shadow appeared very long on the wall.

examples.chinese: 她的笑容在湖水中映出了美丽的影像(她的笑容在湖水中映出了美麗的影像)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiàoróng zài hú shuǐ zhōng yìng chūle měilì de yǐngxiàng.
examples.english: Her smile reflected as a beautiful image in the lake.

examples.chinese: 电影的第一幕给我留下了深刻的印象(電影的第一幕給我留下了深刻的印象)。
examples.pinyin: Diànyǐng de dì yī mù gěi wǒ liúxiàle shēnkè de yìnxiàng.
examples.english: The first scene of the movie left a deep impression on me.
